<Profile>### IDENTITY

- **Age:** 38
- **Gender:** Female
- **Experience level:** **Novice (Beginner)** — new to consistent training (as reported)

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**
  - **Lose weight and improve body composition** (fat loss + improved muscle tone)
- **Secondary goals:**
  - **Increase daily energy** and fitness capacity to keep up with kids
  - Build a consistent, sustainable training habit despite a busy schedule
- **Target events/deadlines:** None provided

---

### TRAINING CONTEXT

#### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** Not specified (schedule described as unpredictable)
- **Desired training frequency:** **3 sessions/week**
- **Session duration preferences/constraints:** Not specified
- **Time windows:** Not specified
- **Blocked days:** Not specified
- **Notes:** Single parent; **very busy** with **limited + unpredictable schedule** → plan should support short, flexible sessions and “minimum effective dose” options.

#### Equipment & Environment
- **Primary location:** Home gym (garage)
- **Available equipment:**
  - **Kettlebells** (exact weights not provided)
  - **Resistance bands** (types/resistances not provided)
- **Environment constraints:** Not specified (space, flooring, noise, etc. unknown)

#### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE] (since 2026-02-27)** Postpartum core issues — needs **core modifications**. Avoid high-pressure abdominal work until tolerance is confirmed; prioritize breath, deep core, and gradual progressions.
- **[ACTIVE] (since 2026-02-27)** Very limited/unpredictable schedule — requires flexible programming, short sessions, and backup workouts.

#### Preferences
- Likes/dislikes: Not provided
- Training style preference: Not provided (will assume efficient full-body sessions unless updated)
- Communication style: Not provided
- Focus areas: Fat loss/body composition + energy

---

### METRICS
*(No baseline performance or body composition data provided yet; placeholders added for consistency.)*

**Body Composition** (priority aligned with goal)
- **Current weight:** Not provided (as of 2026-02-27)
- **Waist/hip measurements:** Not provided (as of 2026-02-27)
- **Body fat %:** Not provided (as of 2026-02-27)

**Strength**
- No current lift/baseline metrics provided (as of 2026-02-27)

**Endurance**
- No baseline cardio metrics provided (as of 2026-02-27)

**Movement Quality**
- Postpartum core status noted, but no functional baseline recorded yet (as of 2026-02-27)

---

### LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (intake)
- Added goals: weight loss + improved body composition; increase energy for kids.
- Training context captured: **3x/week desired**, **home garage gym**, equipment = kettlebells + bands.
- Constraints recorded as **ACTIVE**: postpartum core issues (needs modifications); unpredictable schedule/single parent.
- Metrics not provided yet; body composition + performance baselines pending.</Profile>

reference.response:
## Header
- **Program name:** Busy-Mom Body Recomp (KB + Bands) — Postpartum Core-Safe, 3×/Week
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Linda Martinez
- **Goal:** Lose weight and improve body composition (fat loss + improved muscle tone) while boosting daily energy
- **Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling** (4-week phases)

---

## Program Philosophy
Linda is a novice lifter with an unpredictable schedule and postpartum core considerations, so the program prioritizes **simple full-body sessions** that deliver a “minimum effective dose” for strength, muscle tone, and calorie burn without requiring perfect weekly consistency. A **3-day full-body structure** builds habits and spreads workload so each session still “counts” even if one gets missed. Exercises emphasize **kettlebell basics + bands** (hinge, squat, push, pull, carry) because they are time-efficient, scalable, and ideal for home training. Core training is **postpartum-friendly**: we avoid high-pressure ab work (aggressive sit-ups/crunches, hard planks early on) and instead build capacity using **breath + deep core + anti-rotation/carry patterns**, progressing only when symptoms and control are good. Progress is primarily **reps → load → sets** using RPE (effort) so Linda can autoregulate around sleep, stress, and time.

---

# Phase 1: Foundation & Core-Safe Rebuild (Weeks 1–4)

### Weekly Pattern (3 sessions/week, flexible days)
Aim for **30–40 minutes**. If time is tight, use the built-in **15-minute Minimum Session** at the end of each day.

> **Intensity guideline:** mostly **RPE 6–7** (finish sets with ~3–4 reps in reserve).  
> **Rest:** 45–90 sec between sets; keep it moving but never breath-hold/strain.

---

## Day A — Full Body “Basics + Core Control”
- **Focus:** technique, full-body strength, breath/core control
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Breathing + Core Prep (2–4 minutes)**
- **90/90 breathing with exhale emphasis:** 4–6 slow breaths  
- **Heel slides or marching (supine):** 2 × 6–8/side (slow)  
  *Builds deep core control with low abdominal pressure.*

2) **Kettlebell Deadlift (from floor or elevated if needed):** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 6–7  
   *Foundational hinge for glutes/hamstrings—high return for body composition.*

3) **Incline Push-Up (hands on bench/box) OR Band Chest Press:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 6–7  
   *Push strength without excessive spinal/abdominal pressure.*

4) **Band Row (anchor in door/around post) OR 1-Arm KB Row:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
   *Upper back for posture and shoulder balance.*

5) **Supported Split Squat (hand on wall/bench):** 2–3 × 6–10/side @ RPE 6–7  
   *Single-leg strength with lower load demand (good for home + fatigue management).*

6) **Suitcase Carry (1 KB):** 4 × 20–40 steps/side (moderate)  
   *Anti-lateral flexion core work that’s postpartum-friendly and “functional.”*

---

## Day B — Full Body “Squat + Pull + Carry”
- **Focus:** legs + back, steady effort, low complexity
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Breathing + Core Prep**
- **Crocodile breathing (prone) or 90/90 breathing:** 4–6 breaths  
- **Dead bug (short range, slow exhale):** 2 × 5–8/side  
  *Progress only if you can keep ribs down and avoid doming.*

2) **Goblet Squat (to box if needed):** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 6–7  
   *Simple squat pattern, easy to scale.*

3) **Band Lat Pulldown (anchor high) OR Band Assisted Pull-Down:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 7  
   *Back + lats for upper-body tone and posture.*

4) **KB Floor Press (single or double) OR Band Chest Press:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
   *Pressing strength with stable setup.*

5) **KB Romanian Deadlift:** 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
   *Posterior chain volume without heavy strain.*

6) **Front Rack Carry (KB at chest) OR Farmer Carry (2 KBs if available):** 4 × 20–40 steps  
   *Global trunk stiffness + conditioning without impact.*

---

## Day C — Full Body “Conditioning + Tone” (Low-Impact)
- **Focus:** metabolic work while keeping form; steady breathing
- **Volume:** ~12–16 sets (often done as circuits)

1) **Breathing + Core Prep**
- **Exhale-focused breathing + pelvic floor relaxation on inhale:** 4–6 breaths  
- **Glute bridge:** 2 × 8–12 (pause 1 sec at top)  
  *Glutes support pelvis/trunk control.*

2) **Circuit (repeat 3–4 rounds; rest 60–90 sec between rounds)**
- **KB Deadlift or Hinge:** 8–12 reps @ RPE 6–7  
- **Band Row:** 12–15 reps @ RPE 7  
- **Incline Push-Up or Band Press:** 8–12 reps @ RPE 7  
- **Step-ups (low step) OR Bodyweight Box Squat:** 8–12/side @ RPE 6–7

3) **Core/Finish**
- **Pallof Press (band anti-rotation):** 2–3 × 8–12/side (slow exhale)  
  *Anti-rotation is typically lower pressure than flexion-based ab work.*

---

### Phase 1 Progression Strategy (Weeks 1–4)
- **Week 1:** choose conservative loads; leave reps in the tank (RPE ~6).
- **Weeks 2–3:** use **double progression**:
  - Keep the same weight and add **1–2 reps per set** until you hit the top of the range.
  - When you can hit the **top reps for all sets** at RPE ≤7, increase load next session (smallest jump possible), and return to the lower end of the rep range.
- **Week 4 (Deload / Reset):**
  - Keep exercises the same but cut **volume ~40–50%** (fewer sets), keep effort **RPE 6**.
- **When to move on:** after Week 4, if technique feels stable and core symptoms are calm (no pain, no heaviness/pressure, minimal/no doming), transition to Phase 2.

---

# Phase 2: Build (Strength + Muscle Tone) (Weeks 5–8)

### Weekly Pattern
Same 3-day structure, slightly more challenge and optional density work.

> **Intensity guideline:** main lifts **RPE 7–8**; accessories **RPE 7–8**.

## Day A — Hinge Emphasis + Upper Push/Pull
- **Focus:** glutes/hamstrings + upper strength
- **Volume:** ~16–20 working sets
- **KB Deadlift (heavier):** 4 × 5–8 @ RPE 7–8  
- **KB Floor Press or Push-Up:** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8  
- **1-Arm KB Row or Band Row (heavier):** 4 × 8–12 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Supported Split Squat:** 3 × 8–12/side @ RPE 7  
- **Suitcase Carry:** 4 × 30–60 steps/side

## Day B — Squat Emphasis + Back + Core
- **Focus:** legs + back thickness; core stability
- **Volume:** ~16–20 working sets
- **Goblet Squat:** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Band Lat Pulldown:** 4 × 10–15 @ RPE 7–8  
- **KB RDL:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Band Face Pull / Pull-Apart:** 2–3 × 12–20 @ RPE 7  
  *Shoulder health + posture, helpful for busy parent life.*
- **Pallof Press:** 3 × 10–12/side (slow)

## Day C — Density Day (Time-Efficient Conditioning)
- **Focus:** calorie burn + work capacity without impact
- **Volume:** ~12–18 sets (time-based)
- **20-minute EMOM (Every Minute on the Minute)** — alternate:
  - **Minute 1:** KB hinge (deadlift or swing *only if core control is excellent*) 10–15 reps @ RPE 7
  - **Minute 2:** Push (push-ups or band press) 8–12 reps @ RPE 7
  - **Minute 3:** Pull (band row) 12–15 reps @ RPE 7
  - **Minute 4:** Goblet squat or step-ups 8–12 reps @ RPE 7  
  Repeat 5 cycles (20 min).
- **Carry finisher:** Farmer or front rack carry 4 × 30–60 steps

### Phase 2 Progression Strategy (Weeks 5–8)
- **Weeks 5–7:** progress by **adding load** once you can complete all sets at the top of the rep range with RPE ≤8.
- **Density day:** keep reps the same; improve by:
  - smoother form,
  - slightly heavier KB/band tension,
  - or reduced rest (stay under control).
- **Week 8 deload:** reduce sets by ~40–50%, keep RPE 6–7.

---

# Phase 3: Conditioning + Maintenance of Strength (Weeks 9–12)

### Weekly Pattern
Keep strength work present but bias toward **work capacity** and consistency (useful for fat loss + energy).

## Day A — Strength Maintenance (Full Body)
- **Focus:** keep strength, low-to-moderate volume
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets
- **KB Deadlift:** 3 × 5–8 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Goblet Squat:** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Press (push-up/band/KB):** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Row/Pulldown:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Carry:** 4 × 30–60 steps

## Day B — Conditioning Circuit (Low Impact)
- **Focus:** steady sweat, no maxing
- **Volume:** 25–35 minutes continuous work
- **Circuit (repeat 4–6 rounds):**
  - Hinge 10–12
  - Row 12–15
  - Squat pattern 10–12
  - Press 8–12
  - Pallof press 8–10/side  
  Keep RPE ~7 (you should be able to talk in short sentences).

## Day C — “Optional Interval” Day (Based on Energy)
- **Focus:** metabolic + core-friendly power/endurance
- **Volume:** 15–30 minutes
Choose one:
- **Option 1: 10 rounds**  
  - 30 sec fast step-ups / 30 sec easy pace  
- **Option 2: KB complex (light-moderate)** for 12–15 minutes  
  - deadlift → row → squat → press (5 reps each), rest as needed

### Phase 3 Progression Strategy (Weeks 9–12)
- Maintain or slightly increase loads on strength day if recovery is good.
- For conditioning days, progress by:
  - adding 1 round,
  - adding 2–5 minutes,
  - or slightly increasing resistance—**without** turning it into all-out intensity.
- **Week 12 deload:** reduce total work by ~30–40% and keep everything at RPE 6–7.

---

## Minimum Effective Dose Options (for unpredictable days)
Use these when you only have **10–15 minutes**. They preserve momentum and still drive results.

### 15-Min “Minimum Session” (choose 1)
**Option A (Strength density):** 10 minutes alternating
- KB deadlift × 8–10
- Push-ups or band press × 6–10  
Then **2 carries** (30–40 steps)

**Option B (Full-body circuit):** 12 minutes continuous
- Goblet squat × 8
- Band row × 12
- Glute bridge × 10
- Pallof press × 8/side

**Rule:** stop 1–3 reps shy of form breakdown; no breath-holding.

---

## Phase Cycling
- **Cycle order:** **Phase 1 (Foundation) → Phase 2 (Build) → Phase 3 (Conditioning) → repeat**
- **Transition trigger:** primarily **time-based (every 4 weeks)**, but adjust based on life stress:
  - If sleep/stress is high or schedule collapses: repeat the current phase or run a deload-style week.
  - If Phase 1 core work still feels shaky (doming, pressure, symptoms): **repeat Phase 1** and keep intensity lower.
- **Long-term intent:** each new cycle uses slightly heavier KBs/band tension, cleaner reps, or more total work in the same time—without needing more days per week.

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created. Emphasized 3×/week full-body training with postpartum core-safe progressions, autoregulated RPE, deload every 4th week, and “minimum session” backups to match unpredictable schedule.

--- 

If you tell me (1) what kettlebell weights you have and (2) how many minutes you *usually* can train when it works out (20? 30? 45?), I can tighten the loading targets and pick the best default variations (e.g., whether goblet squats or split squats should be the primary lower-body move).
